items.title: Confident Bangladesh pose Australia a fresh challenge after 15-year wait for an ODI series
items.summary: While spin dominated in Pakistan, Australia could find pace playing more of a role this series

items.title: Pace or spin, onus on batters to meet the challenge
items.summary: Bangladesh's training session proceeded as usual at the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium yesterday, with players first undergoing fielding drills on the centre wicket before moving to the academy ground for net practice.

items.title: Marsh and Head out of Bangladesh ODI series
items.summary: Marsh will, however, fly over to be with the team as he prepares for a comeback in the subsequent Twenty20 series in Bangladesh.

items.title: Travis Head, Mitchell Marsh to miss Bangladesh ODIs
items.summary: While Head has been granted personal leave, Marsh is yet to recover from his ankle injury

items.title: Head out of Bangladesh tour, Marsh to miss ODIs; Murphy called up
items.summary: Head rested from the entire Bangladesh tour while Australia hope Marsh can recover from his ankle injury in time for the T20 series

items.title: Spin-heavy Bangladesh bank on experienced core to survive stiff group
items.summary: This is Bangladesh's most experienced group yet, but Group 1 poses an immense challenge

items.title: Tamim: Building a world-class High Performance Centre 'my biggest dream'
items.summary: The newly elected BCB president also hopes to restore the reputation of Bangladesh cricket

items.title: Tamim vows transparency after being elected BCB president
items.summary: A new Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) elections were held just eight months after the previous one and, as widely expected, Tamim Iqbal was elected unopposed as board president by the newly formed panel of directors on Sunday.

items.title: Tamim Iqbal elected new BCB president
items.summary: The former Bangladesh captain will serve a four-year term, having already completed two months as the president of an ad-hoc committee

items.title: Tamim becomes BCB president uncontested after election sweep
items.summary: Former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal was elected uncontested as president of the Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) after no other candidate challenged his nomination following Sunday's board elections, the BCB Election Commission announced in Mirpur.

items.title: Tamim Iqbal appointed BCB president
items.summary: The former left-handed batter was serving as the interim head of the board after the Bangladesh government dissolved the BCB board of directors in April this year

items.title: Fresh election, familiar pattern
items.summary: Eight months after the last Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) election, the country's cricket governing body is set to hold another board of directors' election today. Yet despite a change in administration, the overall picture appears largely unchanged, making this another largely predictable and excitement-free contest.

items.title: Tigresses suffer heavy defeat in T20 World Cup warm-up
items.summary: Bangladesh national women's team suffered a crushing 68-run defeat in their opening warm-up match of the upcoming ICC Women's T20 World Cup at the Loughborough University, Loughborough on Saturday.

items.title: Women's T20 World Cup 2026 warm-up: Kapp, Wolvaardt take SA past Ireland
items.summary: Scotland thumped Netherlands by 58 runs, Athapaththu helped SL beat Pakistan, and NZ eased past Bangladesh

items.title: Nepal, Malaysia, Hong Kong, Oman qualify for Asian Games men's cricket competition
items.summary: The four teams will join Afghanistan, Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka and Japan in the ten-team competition starting September 24

items.title: Dilara's fastest fifty guides Bangladesh to victory over Netherlands
items.summary: Dilara scored her fifty off 22 balls, the fastest by a Bangladesh batter in women's T20I

items.title: ICC delegation visits Bangladesh to 'review developments' on BCB's governance, electoral matters
items.summary: ICC's Dr Mohammed Moosajee and Tavengwa Mukuhlani will submit their findings and observations to the ICC around the BCB's governance issues

items.title: Shaun Tait resigns as Bangladesh fast bowling coach
items.summary: After 12 months in the role, he is stepping down to prioritise family time

items.title: Kathryn Bryce's all-round effort takes Scotland to tri-series win
items.summary: Scotland finished as the table-toppers after beating Netherlands, with three wins in four matches and one match between Bangladesh and Netherlands to go

items.title: Bangladesh recall Mosaddek after four years for ODIs against Australia
items.summary: Nurul Hasan also returns to the ODI squad for the first two games. Mahidul Islam and Afif Hossain have been left out

items.title: New Zealand home summer to start with blockbuster all-format India tour
items.summary: The New Zealand men's side will also host Sri Lanka for an all-format tour, while the women will host Bangladesh for three T20Is and as many ODIs

items.title: Sultana, Moni help Bangladesh bounce back against Scotland
items.summary: Fast bowler Moni starred with a career-best four-for to trigger Scotland's collapse, in their chase of 153
